figures.caption: Figure 1: Boolean relaxation surface fδ,λ(t) for logistic regression with λ = 0 and varying curvature parameter δ. The surfaces are plotted over the domain t = (t1, t2) ∈ [0, 1]2 with five geometrically increasing values of δ shown in different colours according to the colour bar. As δ increases, the relaxed objective becomes more peaked, driving the solution toward binary corners.

Figure 3: Performance results in the high-dimensional setting (n = 200, p = 1000) for Case 1 (top row) and Case 2 (bottom row). Each panel displays the average over 50 replications as a function of predictor correlation ρ ∈ {0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6}, with vertical bars denoting one standard error.

Table 2: Classification performance on the Khan SRBCT test set (ntest = 20). “Genes” denotes the number of genes selected (out of p = 2,308). Test accuracy is the proportion of correctly classified test samples.

Figure 5: Best-subset inclusion path for the Khan SRBCT gene expression dataset. Each row corresponds to a model size k = 1, . . . , 20, and each filled cell indicates that the corresponding gene is included in the best subset of that size. The test classification accuracy is displayed at the end of each row, with perfect accuracy (100%) highlighted in red. The path exhibits a monotone nesting property: once a gene enters the model, it remains selected at all larger model sizes.

Figure 2: Performance results in the low-dimensional setting (n = 200, p = 30) for Case 1 (top row) and Case 2 (bottom row). Each panel displays the average over 50 replications as a function of predictor correlation ρ ∈ {0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6}, with vertical bars denoting one standard error.

Figure 4: (a) Best-subset inclusion path showing the selected SNPs for different model sizes k = 1 to k = 10. (b) Correlation matrix for the selected SNPs showcasing relationships between the predictors.
